120942.explanation: Referendums are not a solution for complex political issues. Furthermore, with a consultative referendum, the result is not binding, causing confusion and dissatisfaction. A (broad) survey is better if the municipality wants to involve residents' opinions.

120945.explanation: We find it important to assess on a case-by-case basis what is possible. This helps farmers maintain their right to exist.

120946.explanation: A sustainable house is an efficient house. That is good for the climate and your wallet. Therefore, the municipality helps residents through the Energy Desk with the right measures and possible financial options.

120948.explanation: Based on the Environment Act, the municipality creates a program ‘Migrant Labor Housing.’ This program sets housing criteria for initiators or companies that want to deploy migrant laborers.

120949.explanation: We stimulate bee-friendly landscapes and ensure sufficient trees, applying the 3-30-300 green standard. This means from every home three trees are visible, at least 30% of the neighborhood is shaded by trees, and there is green public space within 300 meters of every home.

120952.explanation: If the national fireworks ban proceeds, it is good to offer an alternative to the residents.

120956.explanation: Together with stakeholders, we developed a vision for the future of the countryside and the integration of living, nature, agriculture, energy, and recreation. To that end, we have created the Vital Countryside Program in the past period and are aware of the available agricultural business buildings.

120957.explanation: It is good for children to learn that movement is fun, to set goals, and to work together as a team. Therefore, we want sports to be accessible to everyone, especially youth, without physical and financial barriers.

120961.explanation: Sports clubs often act as the glue in society and deserve support. We promote grassroots sports in Oldebroek. Sports must be available to everyone. Financial support is assessed based on the evaluation framework for initiatives, accommodations, sports, and culture.

120964.explanation: Safe and livable neighborhoods arise when people look out for each other, in cooperation with the neighborhood police officer or BOA as the direct point of contact for residents. For security, collaboration between various parties is very important. This does not happen automatically; the municipality must take the lead.

120965.explanation: Supervision and enforcement are important for safety on the street. We look at obstacles to social safety on the street or in public transport when they form a barrier for people to fully participate. Camera surveillance can help with this, but it is not the first tool to use.

120966.explanation: We focus on shelter and good places for vulnerable people. At the same time, we want to do this especially in cooperation with and support from society.

120970.explanation: Supervision and enforcement against illegal residence in holiday parks/campings is and remains necessary. This is aimed at combating undermining activities.

120972.explanation: Working is not possible for everyone. The municipality ensures that everyone can participate in society according to their abilities and facilitates and encourages volunteer work, occupational day activities, and informal care.

120973.explanation: Our ongoing goal is to promote the self-reliance of every resident. Unfortunately, this is not always possible. We will continue to actively inform our residents about available support options through the municipality’s minimum scheme.

120974.explanation: Our ongoing goal is to promote the self-reliance of every resident. Unfortunately, this is not always possible. We will continue to actively inform our residents about available support options through the municipality’s minimum scheme.

120976.explanation: The number of charging stations is being expanded to enable the growth of electric cars. The NAL (National Charging Infrastructure Agenda) standard can be considered. We advocate for smart charging stations so electric cars can serve as buffers in the electricity network.

120977.explanation: The maximum speed within built-up areas is 30 kilometers per hour. Only on roads with public transport and connecting roads does 50 kilometers per hour apply.

120978.explanation: The choice for care provision is a personal choice. According to the Christian Union, this primarily requires investing in families and communities. The government must stimulate self-care and cooperative care and at the same time ensure that proper professional help is available for truly vulnerable people.

120982.explanation: Tourism is important to us. Many tourists also enjoy our surroundings, and many entrepreneurs benefit from this. Each year, we determine the tourist tax and assess whether we are in line with neighboring municipalities.

120985.explanation: We maintain the urgency status of status holders but want to continue investing in more housing. In doing so, we stand up for our residents by choosing more homes, ensuring flow, affordability, and strengthening local ties. We stand for both mercy and justice.

120989.explanation: Every child in Oldebroek is proficient in swimming by the end of primary school age. For parents who cannot afford swimming lessons for their children, the municipality pays for the lessons through the social minima scheme. School swimming is not necessary for this.

120990.explanation: And because of that, we completely agree! If you grow up with dialect, it's often much easier to express feelings and emotions in dialect to each other. You generally understand each other with very few words.

120993.explanation: High-rise buildings can be an important tool to realize (affordable) homes and business space on a relatively small area without further affecting the countryside. At the same time, high-rise must be carefully integrated and not be possible everywhere.

120994.explanation: We are in favor of creating more housing for our residents, especially young people. We want to prevent only landlords from benefiting through higher rental income in poor conditions. Additionally, homes and neighborhoods are often not designed for multiple tenants at one address.

120995.explanation: The Christian Union stands for livable villages, including the small communities. The cultural offer is of great importance to this livability and a reason for people to want to live somewhere. Therefore, we support a vibrant association life and want to preserve the village community centers in all areas.

120996.explanation: We adhere to the parking standards as we have set them. In some places, more parking spaces are needed due to specific circumstances; then it will be assessed whether this is feasible. We are not in favor of creating more parking spaces in every village.

120998.explanation: We implement the distribution law. Good communication is essential. We want residents to be clearly and timely informed about the arrival of a reception center and for concerns and wishes to be listened to. The council must impose requirements on the COA regarding the conditions of reception.

120999.explanation: The events policy has been evaluated. It resulted in the conclusion that for event safety, it is better to set the end time at 1:00 a.m. with an extension to 2:00 a.m. We agree and want to allow this. Without an obligation to be open or to stay open.

121000.explanation: We like to consider the different options the current building can offer. This can be housing but can also be filled with social functions, as is currently the case with temporary accommodation for Goed Bezig and employees of number 13.

120942.explanation: Not every subject is suitable for a referendum. Let’s start with an experiment.

120945.explanation: Give farmers every opportunity to generate an income in a different way.

120946.explanation: The municipality should facilitate this but not financially.

120948.explanation: If employers can organize accommodation outside their business premises, that should also be allowed.

120949.explanation: Nice idea, but that will probably be difficult for private trees.

120952.explanation: When the nationwide fireworks ban is in effect, this is a sensible measure.

120956.explanation: Give farmers the opportunity to repurpose their business premises when they want to stop their operations. Demolition should be done as compensation.

120957.explanation: There is an existing scheme for the very poorest in our society.

120961.explanation: Sports clubs should be financially independent. However, the municipality can provide loans for new construction plans.

120964.explanation: Especially in the village of Wezep, adding one BOA (special enforcement officer) could work well.

120965.explanation: An easy way to expand supervision where needed.

120966.explanation: It’s better to locate such a shelter farther from home, assuming there are sufficient places in the area.

120970.explanation: Existing policy is that permanent residency is not allowed in holiday homes. However, it would be an appropriate measure to reduce housing shortage.

120972.explanation: No, that obligation cannot be imposed because not everyone is capable of it.

120973.explanation: Can be a useful support for people with a low income.

120976.explanation: Due to the proximity of Natura 2000 and the 500m zone around it, we are cautious about reducing nitrogen emissions. Therefore, promote electric driving.

120977.explanation: Especially in town centers and around schools and other institutions, this promotes traffic safety.

120978.explanation: No, let’s not make it more complicated than it already is to organize sufficient care.

120982.explanation: There is no reason for that.

120985.explanation: They belong to the same urgency category as our own residents in emergency situations. There is a legal obligation to provide housing for status holders one way or another.

120989.explanation: For the sake of the swimming safety of our children.

120990.explanation: Nice idea to pass on regional heritage to our youth.

120993.explanation: If high means more than 5 storeys, I do not agree.

120994.explanation: Can be a simple measure to counteract the housing shortage.

120995.explanation: This is important to stimulate local identity.

120996.explanation: There is no reason for that. However, the municipal parking standard should be reviewed.

120998.explanation: ABO chooses to distribute the legal obligation for reception among the existing larger centers of the municipality.

120999.explanation: The end time should actually be extended.

121000.explanation: That is a good, existing idea.

120942.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses strong participation and consultation, but believes that decisions ultimately belong to the municipal council. In the council, CDA carefully weighs decisions with the greatest interest of our society in mind.

120945.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes that ancillary activities should certainly be made possible. Initiatives are welcome but must fit the environment, landscape, and traffic safety.

120946.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to support local sustainable energy initiatives. This way residents retain control over their energy and we strengthen the community.

120948.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes that everyone has the right to good, appropriate housing. Employers in agriculture and horticulture are responsible for good and decent housing for their seasonal workers.

120949.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to protect and strengthen green spaces within our municipal borders. Greening involves more than just trees. We choose to enhance a green environment in all facets of greening. Planting trees is part of this.

120952.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes that a municipal fireworks show is only possible if safety, support, and costs have been carefully considered.

120956.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes that housing development on agricultural land is only possible if it carefully fits within policy, landscape, environment, and policies established in the council. This approach ensures careful management of our open and characteristic countryside.

120957.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses for everyone to be able to participate in sports, including people with social assistance benefits. When this is financially not possible, the municipality can provide support. Because sport promotes health, participation, and social connection. In this way we build a sporty, healthy community.

120961.explanation: CDA Oldebroek opts to financially support sports clubs when they are struggling. Clubs keep villages livable and are valuable meeting places for our residents.

120964.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses a safe municipality. Sufficient public order officers contribute to safety, livability, and enforcement in the centers.

120965.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes that camera surveillance should only be used if it is necessary and effective and privacy is carefully safeguarded.

120966.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to protect victims of domestic violence and therefore always supports a women's shelter. This can be within this municipality or regionally in another municipality. The goal is to provide a safe place for victims.

120970.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes holiday homes are intended for recreation and are not a solution for the housing shortage. The solution to the housing shortage is building! Additionally, we do support the possibility of temporary exemption to live in a holiday home in unexpected situations.

120972.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes that people with social assistance benefits do meaningful social work to the best of their ability. Participation strengthens self-esteem. As CDA we know that every person in our community is valuable and can and wants to contribute to a better Oldebroek for everyone.

120973.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to maintain the exemption from municipal taxes for residents with a low income.

120974.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to raise the income threshold for poverty schemes, so more people receive timely help.

120976.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to expand charging points for electric cars, fitting with the energy transition mission we have as a municipality and society.

120977.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses road safety and increasing the feeling of safety. Limiting the maximum speed within the centers contributes to this.

120978.explanation: Care is about the person and their dignity. For the CDA it is important that people can receive care that aligns with their faith, culture, or beliefs. This contributes to trust, self-determination, and appropriate care.

120982.explanation: CDA Oldebroek opts for a balanced tourist tax: a fair contribution without discouraging tourism.

120985.explanation: As a municipality we have a legal obligation to house a number of refugees with residence permits. We do this with the established housing regulation. The fair truth is that only a small part is allocated to these refugees.

120989.explanation: CDA primarily sees swimming lessons as a responsibility of parents. When parents cannot bear this financially, there are certainly arrangements to support these families.

120990.explanation: CDA chooses to offer cultural education in various forms. This also includes Veluwe history and dialect. It is up to the schools to provide appropriate educational content here.

120993.explanation: CDA Oldebroek believes that high-rise buildings can contribute to solving the housing shortage within our municipality. Additionally, it helps to keep costs of houses and apartments manageable. Of course, high-rise buildings must fit in and strengthen our community.

120994.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses room rental only under clear conditions, with good supervision of livability.

120995.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses that every village has its own village center as a place for meetings and activities.

120996.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses accessible village centers, with smart solutions and attention to space and living environment. Parking facilities must also be provided.

120998.explanation: The municipality of Oldebroek is legally obliged to accommodate people fleeing war and violence. CDA chooses centralized accommodation at one location, with COA fully responsible for implementation and facilities, so that accommodation is humane and the burden on residents remains limited.

120999.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to extend event end times, also after midnight on Saturdays. Events contribute to a lively community. We ask for mutual understanding and a good balance between enjoying peace and celebrating together.

121000.explanation: CDA Oldebroek chooses to repurpose the old building of De Rank for housing. This can be for starters, but also for elderly or families. We do not make a choice in advance but look at what is possible and where the need is.

120942.explanation: The SGP is a supporter of representative democracy in which elected representatives on behalf of the people control the executive board.

120945.explanation: Activities outside the agricultural sector in the countryside are allowed, provided they do not hinder the environment. For farmers and growers, the municipality pursues a flexible policy, with practical support in permitting and business development.

120946.explanation: The role of the municipality is to facilitate projects that fit the environment and have sufficient support, but not to finance them.

120948.explanation: Providing housing is fine but not mandatory on private property.

120949.explanation: The municipality implements a specific tree policy with attention to existing trees, maintenance, and new planting.

120952.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. As long as there is no definitive ban and there is uncertainty about the implementation and conditions of a fireworks ban, the SGP is not in favor of an organized fireworks show by the municipality.

120956.explanation: Change of use of agricultural buildings must be possible to prevent deterioration and undermining. This means replacing existing buildings and not on valuable agricultural land.

120957.explanation: A generous minimum policy includes exemption from taxes, special social assistance, and health insurance costs. Arrangements are actively communicated by the municipality. There is already a scheme that provides for this. As far as we are concerned, this is sufficient.

120961.explanation: Amateur sports receive support through accessible and sustainable sports facilities.

120965.explanation: Only install cameras incidentally when the situation calls for it.

120966.explanation: This has already been addressed regionally.

120970.explanation: Temporary residence in recreational homes can be allowed to a limited extent, with clear frameworks in the zoning plan.

120972.explanation: Residents receiving benefits provide a counter-performance where possible, for example through volunteer work.

120973.explanation: A generous minimum income policy includes tax relief, special assistance, and health insurance. The municipality actively promotes these schemes.

120974.explanation: The municipality prevents the poverty trap by contributing to making (more) work financially more attractive than receiving benefits.

120976.explanation: The SGP wants sufficient charging stations in public spaces to encourage electric transport. In our view, these are already present.

120977.explanation: We design roads sustainably and safely. Residential areas have a speed limit of 30 km/h. These roads must also meet the guidelines for a safe 30 km/h road in their layout.

120978.explanation: The municipality ensures that the statutory right to freedom of choice for care recipients in youth care is guaranteed in practice, so that they receive care that aligns with their beliefs.

120982.explanation: Raising taxes should not be a goal in itself. The SGP therefore approaches tax increases cautiously. An increase in the tourist tax can be considered if it contributes to qualitative improvements in our municipality and aligns with the level of surrounding municipalities.

120985.explanation: Temporary housing is considered as an option for status holders to reduce pressure on regular housing.

120989.explanation: The SGP’s preference is to provide a nationwide swimming lesson bonus of €1000 per 4-year-old child to attend swimming lessons.

120990.explanation: Schools receive support for nature and cultural education. The municipality gives them space for their own interpretation that fits the school's identity.

120993.explanation: High-rise buildings can be a possible solution if they are well integrated into the environment. But Oldebroek is a village, not a city; therefore no tall residential towers.

120994.explanation: The SGP is open to new forms of housing, but only within clear frameworks.

120995.explanation: Liveability in small villages must be maintained, but that does not mean creating a village center for every village.

120996.explanation: This depends entirely on the situation in the village center. Family parking spaces are created at shopping centers and other busy places.

120998.explanation: The SGP advocates hospitality for refugees, but asylum seeker accommodation must fit the nature and scale of the community. That means no small-scale asylum centers in every part of the Oldebroek municipality.

120999.explanation: Events can make an important contribution to the municipality’s liveliness. The SGP is open to sociable activities that promote social cohesion. The SGP would like to set event end times at 11:00 PM. This contributes to health, liveability, and enjoyment of living for all residents of Oldebroek.

121000.explanation: We are in favor of a good mix of housing, not only for starters.

120942.explanation: We prefer that residents are involved from the outset in planning! We find it important that residents have a say. But a referendum with only yes or no often does not work well. It can set people against each other.

120945.explanation: We think it’s good if farmers earn extra income with a shop or campsite. It must of course fit the surroundings. And neighbors should not be disturbed.

120946.explanation: We want residents to produce clean energy together, for example with solar panels or a neighborhood battery. The municipality may well help with this. This keeps energy in the village and saves people money on their bills.

120948.explanation: If you have people working, you must also ensure they can live well. This is the employer’s responsibility in seasonal work. This also prevents additional housing shortages for other residents. The housing must be proper and safe. Therefore, supervision must be strict!

120949.explanation: Trees are important. They provide shade, clean air, and a pleasant environment. If a tree has to be removed, a new one must be planted in its place. Preferably more than one. More greenery makes Oldebroek more beautiful and healthier.

120952.explanation: A central fireworks show is safer and better for animals and the environment than fireworks everywhere. This way everyone can enjoy a beautiful show without nuisance and accidents in the neighborhood. It also brings people together.

120956.explanation: We need housing, so we are not negative about this. But we also want to protect our beautiful landscape. Building on farmland should therefore be assessed case by case. It must fit the surroundings.

120957.explanation: Sports are good for your health and you meet other people. Money should not be a reason to be unable to play sports. We believe everyone should be able to participate, including people with little money.

120961.explanation: Sports clubs are the heart of our villages. Volunteers commit themselves to the club and the neighborhood. If a club encounters problems, the municipality may help. Sports keep people healthy and bring them together.

120964.explanation: We want enforcement officers (BOAs) to be visible in the villages, near schools and shopping centers. They provide a safe feeling and can intervene quickly in case of nuisance. More enforcement officers help keep our municipality safe and liveable.

120965.explanation: Cameras can help solve problems, but they are not a miracle solution. We also value privacy. Cameras should be placed only where really necessary and where other solutions don’t work.

120966.explanation: People who are not safe at home must have somewhere to go. Domestic violence occurs everywhere, including in our municipality. A safe place nearby can save lives. We believe victims deserve good shelter. Speed and accessibility are the most important factors!

120970.explanation: There is a housing shortage, so we understand that people look at this. But holiday homes are often not suitable for year-round living. And recreation is also important for our municipality. Case by case review is needed.

120972.explanation: Volunteer work can be good for people: you meet others and do something useful. But making it mandatory often backfires. We believe in offering opportunities and helping people. Tailor-made solutions are better than obligations for everyone. If someone can but does not want to, we would like to see more 'nudging'.

120973.explanation: People with little money already have a hard time. Relief helps them keep their heads above water. We believe the strongest shoulders should bear the heaviest burdens, not the other way around.

120974.explanation: Too many people now just fall outside the rules, while they also have difficulties. By raising the threshold, more people can receive help when needed. This prevents people from falling into greater problems.

120976.explanation: More and more people drive electric vehicles. There must be sufficient charging points for that. We want to make sustainable transport easier. More charging stations help achieve this.

120977.explanation: Slower traffic is safer, especially for children and the elderly. At 30 km/h, accidents are less severe. We consider traffic safety important, especially around schools and village centers. This fits with those goals.

120978.explanation: Good care is care you feel comfortable with. For some people, it’s important that a care provider understands their background. We value freedom of choice in care, as long as the quality is good.

120982.explanation: Tourists make use of our beautiful municipality. A slightly higher contribution is reasonable. We can invest that money in nature, culture, and facilities that also benefit our own residents.

120985.explanation: People with a residence permit have the right to a home. But we also understand that our own residents are waiting a long time. We want to build more homes so there is room for everyone, instead of pitting groups against each other.

120989.explanation: Swimming is an important skill, especially in a water-rich country. Every child must be able to learn to swim, even if parents cannot afford it. School swimming assists with that. We therefore support those in need where necessary.

120990.explanation: Dialect belongs to our region and our identity. It's nice if children can experience some of it. It doesn't need to be mandatory, but offering the opportunity is valuable to us. We should cherish culture and heritage.

120993.explanation: We are open to building upwards where it fits the surroundings. Tall buildings can help create more homes without building over all the greenery. But it must suit the environment; high apartment towers do not fit.

120994.explanation: Room rental can help against housing shortages, especially for youth and starters. But it must not cause nuisance to neighbors. Good regulations are needed. Each situation should be assessed individually.

120995.explanation: A village center is the place where people meet. It strengthens the community. We believe in facilities nearby. Every village deserves a heart where residents come together.

120996.explanation: We prioritize cyclists and pedestrians. More parking spaces often means less greenery. Preferably smart parking on the edge and pleasant walking and cycling routes. More parking only if there is really no other option.

120998.explanation: We stand for humane shelter for people in need. Small-scale works better than large locations: it fits the capacity of villages and gives people more chances to participate. Good guidance and balance are important.

120999.explanation: Eleven o'clock is very early for parties and festivals. These belong to a lively municipality. We believe there should be room for entertainment, with good agreements on noise and nuisance. No rigid rules, but tailor-made solutions.

121000.explanation: Vacancy is a shame and starters need homes. Converting an old school building into apartments is smart reuse. This gives the building a new future and helps young people get a home.

120942.explanation: The VVD Oldebroek supports citizen participation to get a better understanding of residents' opinions instead of just a yes or no through referendums.

120945.explanation: The VVD Oldebroek is positive towards secondary activities at agricultural companies, such as farm shops and recreation, as long as they fit the environment and do not cause unnecessary nuisance. Entrepreneurial space with common sense.

120946.explanation: VVD Oldebroek prefers supporting residents' initiatives by removing obstacles and fees rather than setting up new large-scale municipal subsidy schemes, to keep municipal finances healthy.

120948.explanation: VVD Oldebroek supports the principle that agricultural entrepreneurs are responsible for housing seasonal workers on their own premises, provided this is done within clear frameworks and feasibility for the entrepreneur is central.

120949.explanation: VVD Oldebroek is against mandatory replanting for every tree cut down, but supports replanting when it concerns valuable trees or municipal projects.

120952.explanation: VVD Oldebroek does not see organizing a fireworks show as a municipal task but as an unnecessary expense that reduces residents’ freedom of choice. VVD Oldebroek values traditions but also recognizes the problems of illegal fireworks.

120956.explanation: VVD Oldebroek views self-building by retiring farmers on their own land where possible as an opportunity to speed up housing construction and support the agricultural sector, as long as rules remain workable for residents and entrepreneurs.

120957.explanation: VVD Oldebroek supports the principle that sports should be accessible to everyone but believes the municipality should help through current minimum income schemes. The VVD opposes blindly taking over all sports costs to keep municipal finances healthy.

120961.explanation: VVD Oldebroek will not quickly choose to cover gaps for associations during the 2026-2030 council period but prefers structural relief of charges and improving facilities to make associations more resilient.

120964.explanation: VVD Oldebroek is not against more community enforcement officers (boa’s), provided funding is responsible and deployment directly contributes to a safer Oldebroek municipality.

120965.explanation: VVD Oldebroek supports more camera surveillance at locations where it demonstrably contributes to safety, only if this goes hand in hand with responsible use of municipal funds and citizens’ privacy.

120966.explanation: VVD Oldebroek does not advocate for a women's shelter within Oldebroek municipality itself but focuses on strong regional shelters to provide victims with necessary safety and anonymity.

120970.explanation: VVD Oldebroek is cautious about generally allowing permanent residence in holiday homes. The VVD greatly values tourism.

120972.explanation: VVD Oldebroek supports that people on social assistance do socially useful work that is indeed possible. The VVD believes a benefit should not be a dead end and that providing a social contribution helps return to the labor market.

120973.explanation: VVD Oldebroek wants to maintain debt forgiveness for those who really need it but strongly opposes expanding this scheme. The VVD wants the focus on keeping taxes low for all Oldebroek residents.

120974.explanation: VVD Oldebroek chooses to maintain current boundaries and prefers to focus on reducing charges for all citizens to prevent people from falling into poverty in the first place.

120976.explanation: VVD Oldebroek is moderately positive about expanding the number of charging points but prioritizes residents’ needs and preserving parking capacity. The VVD wants to avoid unnecessarily burdening public parking spaces with empty charging spots.

120977.explanation: VVD Oldebroek chooses a tailored approach: 30 km/h where necessary (residential areas, schools), but 50 km/h where possible (through roads) to keep the municipality accessible.

120978.explanation: VVD Oldebroek believes the municipality should provide freedom of choice through means like personal budgets (PGB), but the municipality must prioritize quality of care above faith, culture, or beliefs.

120982.explanation: VVD Oldebroek opposes a high tourist tax. The VVD chooses a moderate and stable rate to support the local tourism sector and keep the municipality attractive for visitors.

120985.explanation: VVD Oldebroek does not want priority for refugees with residence permits (status holders) in social rental housing allocation. The VVD aims for a fairer distribution so that our own residents are not displaced. The VVD will comply with legal obligations.

120989.explanation: VVD Oldebroek opts for targeted support to people with low incomes and maintaining local facilities but believes that paying for swimming lessons for every child is not a municipal task. Also undesirable and unfeasible for schools.

120990.explanation: VVD Oldebroek is positive towards initiatives that contribute to maintaining local culture and identity but believes the decision should lie with the schools themselves and must not lead to extra administrative burdens or high costs.

120993.explanation: VVD Oldebroek chooses quality over quantity in building height. Tall apartment buildings must not damage the village character of the municipality. Each project must be carefully assessed for feasibility.

120994.explanation: VVD Oldebroek is not opposed to allowing room rental provided it contributes to housing needs of starters and students without disturbing neighborhood livability.

120995.explanation: VVD Oldebroek supports the idea that each village has its own functional heart, provided local entrepreneurs are given space, residents want it, and the village's own identity is respected.

120996.explanation: VVD Oldebroek favors good accessibility of town centers but opts for more efficient use of existing parking spaces rather than random creation of new spots. The VVD wants parking to remain clear and free.

120998.explanation: VVD Oldebroek supports small-scale shelter provided care, safety, and social facilities are well guaranteed. Community participation is very important here. VVD Oldebroek wants to comply with legal obligations.

120999.explanation: VVD Oldebroek wants Saturday evening events to responsibly continue later into the evening to keep the villages lively and attractive for young and old. The end time should be gradual to prevent nuisance.

121000.explanation: VVD Oldebroek sees De Rank as a prime example of good spatial planning: it gives an old building a second life and directly helps solve the housing shortage among local youth.

120942.explanation: Agreed. Too often in recent years, the municipality has not properly listened to the will of our residents, as seen in the AZC dossier. A referendum is a good corrective measure to improve our local democracy.

120945.explanation: Excellent to facilitate such wonderful initiatives as a municipality. But above all, the government must pursue a stable agricultural policy. Farmers should be able to do what they are good at, which is farming. They must be able to earn a living from it.

120946.explanation: "Sustainable energy" for the current (local) government means only wind and solar. Residents may install solar panels on their roofs on their own initiative, but we oppose solar fields and wind turbines that damage our landscape.

120948.explanation: Good option, but not mandatory from the local government.

120949.explanation: We strongly support 'green' initiatives and the planting and preservation of trees, but a strict one-to-one replacement rule is too rigid and unnecessary.

120952.explanation: No, a fireworks show is beautiful, but it's silly to shoot tax money into the air for that. Associations can organize and finance this themselves together with residents if there is demand.

120956.explanation: Agreed, we want space for farmers. When demolishing, we want a maximum of 4 houses to be rebuilt. However, if the farmer's land borders the core of a village and falls within a preferred area, the farmer must also have the opportunity for large-scale development themselves.

120957.explanation: In the municipality of Oldebroek, low-income people can already apply for an activities contribution (children €200 & adults €400). That is already very generous. Fully paying for a membership costs too much and is also unfair to other residents who pay it themselves.

120961.explanation: Associations are the engine of our society. They must not collapse in times of need. We monitor finances closely but step in when viable associations face urgent needs.

120964.explanation: Safety is the core task of the government, and thus also for the municipality of Oldebroek. But above all, the national government must strengthen the police force, which is currently lacking.

120965.explanation: We do not want to cover all our villages with cameras. No 'Big Brother is watching you' from the municipality. In places where (after an incident) camera surveillance is needed, it can be useful, provided the municipality justifies it well and pursues temporariness.

120966.explanation: More broadly, the government must provide temporary housing for people in crisis.

120970.explanation: Not permanently, but in times of extreme housing shortages, temporary tolerance can be an option in some cases. This is now even the line of the national government (Minister Keijzer). Oldebroek should also consider this.

120972.explanation: You get what you give. Those living at the community's expense should definitely deliver something in return.

120973.explanation: There are already many local provisions for the minimum income group. Completely waiving municipal taxes is unfair to other residents.

120974.explanation: Not upfront. This has major financial implications, and given the budget, it is not unthinkable that taxes would need to be raised as a result.

120976.explanation: The municipality already wants to expand this significantly, and not everyone can afford an electric car. Moreover, there are major problems with grid congestion, and this comes at the expense of regular parking spaces.

120977.explanation: This can be useful in places where traffic safety demands it, but not as a standard everywhere.

120978.explanation: Care is not just an act or transaction but valuable human work. Identity plays a large role in this. The municipality must ensure that residents who want it can choose Christian, identity-based care. However, we do not consider Islamic care.

120982.explanation: This was already raised once in 2019, and we definitely do not want to raise it further upfront. We have a thriving tourism sector that we do not want to stifle from the municipality by higher tourist taxes.

120985.explanation: This is unfair and, in our eyes, even pure discrimination against our own local residents. CVO has made multiple attempts to abolish this but to no avail. After the elections, CVO will – hopefully with more seats – try again to remove this priority.

120989.explanation: In a watery area, swimming skills are our top priority. A personal contribution is obviously desired, but swimming development must also be accessible to every child and not only to those who can pay.

120990.explanation: CVO is committed to cherishing our local identity. We have also submitted an amendment recently in local cultural policy for this. Our dialect is a gem we want to cherish. That’s who we are!

120993.explanation: CVO is critical of high-rise buildings that harm the rural character of our villages. We say: in principle a maximum of 3 residential floors, and a maximum of 4 if it fits the environment. High residential flats (5 floors or more) we should not want.

120994.explanation: In times of housing shortage, we must embrace creative solutions to realize more homes within our municipality. Renting out individual rooms and splitting homes are good options for this.

120995.explanation: Every village deserves facilities and a center where people come together. This will naturally differ between small and large villages, but we support this principle. Village halls and schools are crucial here.

120996.explanation: Parking spaces are increasingly under pressure in our municipality, among other things due to recent plans by the council at Lage Weide, Oldebroek Center Plan, and Mariënrade Wezep. Many people in this area depend on their car. Therefore, we must focus on sufficient parking spaces.

120998.explanation: We are against 'musts' and therefore against any form of asylum coercion or pressure. We oppose any form of large-scale asylum reception. Small-scale reception is only discussable if there is support and capacity among our residents.

120999.explanation: CVO proposes to lift closing times on Friday evenings and set those on Saturday at 00:00. This way, events can continue longer and still go hand in hand with Sunday rest.

121000.explanation: Participation is the key word here. There has been much uncertainty about The Rank plans, after which CVO asked the council for more clarity. Good cooperation with current residents is essential for a new plan to succeed.

statements.title: The end time for all events in Oldebroek should be 11:00 PM on Saturdays.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

titleParts.text: in Oldebroek moet op zaterdagen 23.00 uur worden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Oldebroek needs more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The residents of Oldebroek should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Oldebroek moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: More high-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Oldebroek.
More CCTV surveillance should be installed in Oldebroek.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The old building of primary school De Rank should be converted into an apartment complex for first-time buyers.

titleParts.text: Het oude gebouw van basisschool De Rank moet worden omgebouwd tot een appartementencomplex voor

titleParts.glossary: People buying a house for the first time who have never had a mortgage.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Veluws dialect.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: A women's shelter for victims of domestic violence should be established in Oldebroek.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
There should be small-scale asylum reception facilities in the villages/settlements of the municipality of Oldebroek.

titleParts.glossary: Places where only a few people are housed together. Not many people in one place.
A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).
Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.
